domain: campanology (change-ringing Plain Bob, extents as Hamiltonian cycles in Cayley digraphs of S_n)
specific_mechanism: A 'method' (e.g. Plain Bob Minimus on 4 bells) is a procedure that traverses every permutation of n bells exactly once, returning to the start. Each ringer memorizes a path; the collective produces a Hamiltonian cycle on the Cayley digraph of S_n with generating set the allowed-transition involutions. Plain Bob's cleverness: a small, locally-defined per-ringer rule generates a globally-coherent permutation enumeration.
llm_application: Prompt-space enumeration via locally-defined per-token rewrite rules that globally produce a Hamiltonian-style cover of the candidate prompt space. Each rewrite-rule analog of a ringer's local pattern is bounded to two adjacent positions; the composition produces a sequence of prompts that visits each variant exactly once without repetition. Null-space-traversal: the rule set spans the null-space of an equivalence relation on prompts (perm-invariant under a chosen symmetry) and provides a coverage guarantee that beats random sampling or grid search.
novelty_claim: Prompt-space complexity analyses (2503.10084 ACL 2025), Reich et al. 1500-prompt search, Sequential Enumeration in LLMs (2512.04727), Combinatorial-optimization-with-LLMs review (2507.03637) — none specifically uses Cayley-graph Hamiltonian-cycle change-ringing methods (or local-rule generators) for prompt enumeration with coverage guarantees.
